reasoning: The Kansas City Chiefs are attempting to win the AFC West in the 2025-2026 season. They entered the season as a moderate favorite to win the division at around -115 to -125 (approx 53-55% implied probability), reflecting a tighter expected race compared to their previous dominant years. They started the 2025 season poorly, going 0-2 and dealing with early-season struggles (including a loss to the Jaguars that dropped them to 2-3). However, updates as of late October indicate they have righted the ship: they are fully healthy, recently got standout receiver Rashee Rice back, and and have won two straight games to reach 4-3. They currently sit 1 game behind the division-leading Denver Broncos (5-2) and are tied with the Chargers (4-3). Although they have fallen a game behind the Broncos, they are considered by models and oddsmakers to be roughly a 2-win better true-talent team over a full season, meaning they are expected to make up this 1-game deficit over the remaining 10 games. Consequently, the Division race is virtually a coin-flip between the Chiefs and the field (primarily the Broncos and Chargers). I have estimated the Chiefs' updated probability to be slightly below their pre-season 54% due to the 1-game deficit, landing at 44%.
